name: Vanilla Forums Community Platform API
what_it_does: Vanilla Forums enterprise community platform REST API for brands and enterprises to build and manage online communities, support forums, and brand communities with gamification, SSO, advanced moderation, and knowledge base integration. Enables AI agents to manage discussion and post creation for community content automation, handle user registration and profile management for community member automation, access category and tag structure management for content organization automation, retrieve moderation queue and spam management for community safety automation, manage badge and reputation system for gamification automation, handle knowledge base article management for self-service support automation, access analytics and community health metrics for engagement tracking automation, retrieve SSO user provisioning for enterprise identity automation, manage group and role permission for community governance automation, and integrate Vanilla Forums with Salesforce, Zendesk, Google Analytics, and enterprise platforms for end-to-end community management workflow.
use_cases: Building brand communities from enterprise community management agents
Managing support forums from customer self-service agents
Moderating user content from community safety agents
Creating knowledge bases from self-service support agents
not_for: Developer open-source communities (use Discourse for open-source community)
Real-time team chat (use Slack or Teams for internal communication)
Paid gated membership communities (use Circle or Mighty Networks)
best_when: An enterprise brand wanting AI agents to automate community moderation, knowledge base management, gamification, SSO provisioning, and community analytics through Vanilla Forums' enterprise community platform.
avoid_when: HIGHER FIVESTAR PLAN REQUIRED FOR API ACCESS: Vanilla Forums API access requires Higher Logic (parent company) Fivestar plan; lower-tier plans have limited or no API access; automated community management workflows must verify plan includes API access before building integrations. HIGHER LOGIC ACQUISITION PLATFORM CHANGES: Vanilla Forums was acquired by Higher Logic in 2019 and merged with Tomoson/HiveBrite in ongoing product consolidation; long-term API stability and roadmap may shift as Higher Logic consolidates platforms; automated workflows should monitor for migration announcements. GDPR CONTENT DELETION REQUIRES CAREFUL AUTOMATION: Vanilla Forums supports GDPR right-to-erasure for user data; automated user deletion workflows must coordinate with post anonymization or deletion; automated user deletion without post handling creates orphaned content from deleted users.

auth.notes: Vanilla Forums uses API key and OAuth 2.0 for integrations. REST API with JSON. Montreal, Canada HQ. Founded 2009 by Mark O'Sullivan. Acquired by Higher Logic in 2019. Products: Community forum, knowledge base, gamification, SSO, moderation, analytics. Integrations: Salesforce, Zendesk, Marketo, HubSpot, Google Analytics. GDPR. SOC2. Serves 500+ enterprise brands. Competes with Discourse, Khoros, and Insided for enterprise community platforms.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — enterprise sales process
pricing.paid_tiers: Plus, Fivestar, VIP tiers; custom enterprise pricing; annual contract
pricing.notes: Montreal CA. Higher Logic subsidiary. Custom enterprise pricing. Annual contract. API requires Fivestar plan.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; discussion and user queries throttled; webhooks for post, user, and moderation events

known_agent_gotchas: GARDEN FRAMEWORK CUSTOM ENDPOINT EXTENSIONS: Vanilla Forums uses Garden PHP framework with plugin-based endpoint extensions; automated integrations may encounter custom endpoints added by plugins not documented in core API reference; automated integration must verify endpoint availability for custom Vanilla Forums deployment

SSO COOKIE AUTHENTICATION vs API KEY: Vanilla Forums supports both SSO cookie-based authentication and API key; automated integrations using SSO cookies encounter session expiry; automated workflows should use API key authentication to avoid session management complexity

DISCUSSION vs COMMENT HIERARCHY FOR CONTENT RETRIEVAL: Vanilla Forums organizes content as Discussions (threads) and Comments (replies); automated content retrieval must query both discussion and comment endpoints; automated content aggregation using only discussion endpoint misses all reply/comment content

CATEGORY PERMISSION INHERITANCE FOR AUTOMATED MODERATION: Vanilla Forums category permissions inherit from parent categories; automated moderation workflows targeting subcategories must understand permission inheritance; automated moderation bypass using category ID without permission context may encounter unexpected permission errors

BADGE AWARD AUTOMATION REQUIRES BADGE CONFIGURATION: Vanilla Forums badge system uses configurable award criteria; automated badge award via API requires pre-configured badge definitions; automated gamification without badge configuration creates award request for undefined badge type
agent_readiness.security_notes: Enterprise community. GDPR, SOC2. API key/OAuth2. US/EU. Community, user, and moderation data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Enterprise community. GDPR, SOC2. API key/OAuth2. US/EU. Community and user data.
